title: Clashes erupt during Ram Navami procession in Bengal's Murshidabad, security tightened
content: The situation turned volatile after an altercation during the procession escalated into violence, with both sides throwing stones at each other and vandalising property in the area

title: 5 India-bound LPG ships stay anchored near Hormuz Strait
content: Appearing to bunch together so they can troop out of the strait, these ships carry more than 1.7 lakh tonnes of LPG

title: Chhattisgarh High Court says signs, gestures are oral evidence, upholds conviction of man for raping disabled woman
content: Court observes that the trial court had adopted demonstrative methods, including the use of a plastic doll, to remove any possibility of misunderstanding in the survivor’s testimony

title: ECI suspends police officer for failing to tackle BJP-Trinamool Congress clashes
content: The poll panel described it as "serious negligence and dereliction of duty" on the part of the officer.

title: Candidates take offbeat route to connect with voters in Bengal
content: From kitchen visits to barber shop stunts, candidates across parties attempt unconventional voter connect ahead of the West Bengal Legislative Assembly election

title: Trade deal with India not far-off, but there are still gaps: U.S. official
content: One of the gaps in India-U.S. discussions, as per the U.S. official, is around pulses, with India seeking to safeguard the market for this agricultural commodity and the U.S. wanting greater access to it

title: NIA granted 45-day extension in Red Fort blast probe
content: The NIA, in its plea, cited the need for further investigation in light of the recent arrests of Zamir Ahmed Ahangar and Tufail Ahmad Bhat

title: IIT Guwahati team develops energy-efficient bricks
content: The researchers said these bricks have been designed to keep buildings naturally cool, offering a solution for sustainable construction

title: T.N. government releases draft State Mental Healthcare Regulations, 2026
content: The Tamil Nadu State Mental Health Authority will maintain a register of practising mental health professionals in the State that will be updated monthly. It also proposes to publish a district-wise list of mental health professionals on January 1 every year

title: ED charges ex-RCOM executive Punit Garg in ₹40,000-crore bank loan fraud case
content: Chargesheet also names Vaishali Jairam Mane as an accused, for her alleged involvement in the purchase of a luxury Manhattan apartment from the proceeds of the crime

title: Supreme Court flags repeated breaches of law on disclosure of sexual assault survivors’ identity
content: Supreme Court warns of “general indifference” as case records reveal disclosure of sexual assault survivors’ identity, violating IPC Section 228A

title: Stronger trade corridors and supply chains needed, says Jaishankar
content: External Affairs Minister stresses importance of resilient trade corridors, such as the IMEC, especially amidst uncertainties of West Asian conflict; calls for freedom of navigation to ensure global economic security

title: BJD demands VAT reduction on petrol, diesel in Odisha
content: BJD spokesperson Lenin Mohanty said the ongoing conflict involving Israel, the United States and Iran has triggered concerns of a global fuel crisis

title: People spreading rumours on lockdown will be booked: CM Fadnavis
content: He emphasised that we have enough stock of petrol and diesel to last for a month and appealed to citizens not to line up outside the petrol pump

title: T.N. Assembly election: EC orders State government to post new SPs for 4 districts, including Kancheepuram
content: It adds that the officers transferred out must not be posted in any poll-related posts till the completion of the election

title: Karnataka bypolls: Congress rebel candidate retires from contest in Davangere South
content: Rebel candidate Sadiq Pahilwan announced that he would extend full support to Congress candidate Samarth Mallikarjun, son of Minister S.S. Mallikarjun

title: Karnataka High Court quashes FIR against Sri Sri Ravishankar on land encroachment allegations
content: There is not even an iota of allegation against the petitioner for land grabbing or encroachment, observes Justice M. Nagaprasanna

title: T.N. Assembly election: AIADMK fields new face in Sengottaiyan’s stronghold of Gobichettipalayam
content: V.B. Prabhu, who joined the party in 2002, rose through the ranks to serve as deputy secretary of the party’s student wing for a decade. Over the past six months, he was the West Union secretary in the constituency

title: TVK chief Vijay accuses Chennai police, officials of denying permission for his Perambur campaign
content: “Every time I plan an election campaign, the DMK government continues to delay or deny permission citing various reasons,” he stated.

title: Govt. notifies TRP-2026; tightens audit norms, expands sample size
content: Under the new policy, the net-worth requirement for a company willing to register as TV rating agency has been reduced from existing ₹20 crore to ₹5 crore

title: T.N. Assembly election: AIADMK releases second list of candidates, retains 30 sitting legislators
content: Prominent among those who did not figure in the list are former Ministers V. Saroja, V.M. Rajalakshmi, Vaigaichelvan, and ‘Mafoi’ K. Pandiarajan, all of whom lost in the 2021 election

title: 5 killed as avalanche hits Srinagar-Leh National Highway
content: Officials said the avalanche hit the Srinagar-Leh national highway near Zero Point at the Zojila Pass in the afternoon and buried some vehicles

title: Aditya Birla Group-led consortium acquires Royal Challengers Bengaluru in a $1.76 billion deal
content: It has been sold to a consortium comprising Aditya Birla Group, The Times of India Group, Bolt Ventures, and Blackstone’s perpetual private equity strategy, BXPE in an all-cash deal worth $1.76 billion

title: WoW Awards 2026: The Hindu celebrates women across various fields
content: Achievers from multiple spheres, ranging from healthcare to rural development, were felicitated and the immense contributions they made, breaking barriers along the way, were acknowledged

title: 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly election will be a close battle, says P. Chidambaram
content: Congress leader P. Chidambaram says 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly election will not be one-sided like 2024 Lok Sabha polls; rules out inevitability of coalition government

title: TG Transco urges Irrigation department to clear dues of ₹33,653 crore
content: The dues mostly pertain to period April 1, 2021 to February 28, this year on account of energy, O&M charges, capital works

title: Indian Navy’s IOS SAGAR completes key training phase, boosts maritime cooperation in IOR
content: A key highlight was the extensive use of advanced simulators and modern training infrastructure, enabling realistic, scenario-based learning and enhancing operational readiness in complex maritime situations

title: Service sector fuels Telangana’s explosive per capita income growth in 10 years
content: Though Telangana witnessed a sharp rise in per capita income led by districts like Mahabubnagar and Rangareddy, the gains remain uneven with most districts lagging behind the State average, highlighting persistent regional disparities despite strong service-led economic growth across the State

title: Marumagal Vaaku: an allegorical Tamil short story on the emotional coercion of voters
content: Unlike high-pitched electioneering the State is known for, the TVK’s campaign seems in part to be unfolding quietly within households. What unfolds is reminiscent of the short story Marumagal Vaaku (Dauther-in-law’s Vote) by the late Tamil writer Krishnan Nambi

title: Capturing the political acumen of DMK men in a short story
content: Vaaku Porukkikal  is a short story by Sahitya Akademi Award-winning writer Nanjil Nadan that explores how the then DMK  leaders and candidates represented the aspirations of the people, leading to the victory of the party in 1967

title: PM calls for ‘Team India’ effort in meet with CMs over West Asia crisis
content: He recalls united response during ‘COVID-19 pandemic, when the Centre and States worked together as ‘Team India’ to mitigate the impact on supply chains, trade, and daily life

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Pinarayi pandering to minority and majority communalism to gain a few votes, says Satheesan
content: The Leader of the Opposition alleges that the CM, who had resorted to minority appeasement during the Lok Sabha elections, has quickly changed tack to majority appeasement

title: District election officials identify 416 vulnerable polling booths in Vellore, nearby districts
content: Of the identified vulnerable booths, Vellore has the highest number with 137 booths, followed by Ranipet, Tiruvannamalai, and Tirupattur

title: Hoping to paper cracks in BRICS over war, New Delhi sends out invitations for FMs’ meet and Summit
content: Iran-UAE tensions over strikes, U.S. boycott of South Africa at G-7, G-20 pose diplomatic challenges for India

title: In reset of bilateral ties, Bangladesh’s Foreign Minister expected to visit India in April
content: The visit will be the first high-level outreach from Bangladesh since its Prime Minister Tarique Rahman came to power last month; the two sides could also hold talks on renewing the Ganga Waters Treaty besides exploring ways to manage the energy crisis owing to the war in West Asia

title: Elon Musk joined Trump's call with PM Modi on Iran war earlier this week: Report
content: The conversation focused on the evolving situation in West Asia, with both leaders emphasising the importance of keeping the Strait of Hormuz open, secure and accessible. The key maritime corridor handles a significant share of global oil shipments and remains critical to international energy markets. However, it remains unclear why Musk was included in the call or whether he spoke.

title: Govt slashes excise duty on petrol, diesel to shield consumers, oil companies
content: The windfall tax will also help the Centre partly offset the impact of the excise duty cut. Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs chairman Vivek Chaturvedi said the windfall tax on exports will result in a gain of about Rs 1,500 crore in the first fortnight while the govt will have to forgo over Rs 7,000 crore in revenue because of the excise duty cut. He said the export tax will be reviewed fortnightly.

title: 12 lakh on Bengal’s 2nd supplementary list, but no clarity on who made the cut
content: As was the case when Election Commission released the first supplementary rolls close to midnight last Monday, there were more questions than CEO Manoj Agarwal had answers for. “About 37 lakh of the 60 lakh pending cases have been disposed of till now. After receiving the list from Calcutta HC, EC will take 4-6 hours to process and upload it on our server,” he said in the afternoon.

title: Unite to face West Asia crisis, PM tells CMs; urges to boost people’s confidence
content: While he focused on preparing for prolonged disruption of supply lines, Modi also conveyed assurance about the country being in good stead, saying “efforts...in recent years to strengthen India’s economic and supply systems have proved beneficial”. In his virtual meeting with CMs — except those of Assam, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Puducherry where assembly polls are due next month — on Friday, PM Modi said while addressing a challenge is a shared responsibility.

title: FBI responds to Iran-backed group’s hacking of Director Kash Patel’s personal email
content: Iranian-linked hackers, Handala Hack Team, have breached FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email, releasing personal photos and documents. While the FBI confirmed the breach, they downplayed its significance, stating no government information was compromised. The group, believed to be a front for Iranian intelligence, previously claimed responsibility for hacking medical equipment manufacturer Stryker.

title: Jewar in the crown: PM to open NCR’s 2nd international airport today
content: The PM is scheduled to arrive at the airport at 11.30am on Saturday, go on a walkthrough of the terminal building, and formally inaugurate the airport around noon before addressing a public gathering. The event is expected to draw large crowds from Noida, Greater Noida, Ghaziabad, Bulandshahr and other parts of western UP. Three pandals have been set up to accommodate more than one lakh people.

title: Lockdown reports‘harmful, baseless’, says govt, allays fear
content: Home minister Shah accused opposition parties of spreading panic over the West Asia crisis, including the claim that a lockdown may be imposed. “I want to assure everyone. Do not fear, nothing will happen. We have adequate reserves of petroleum products,” he said. While many countries had taken emergency measures, India was working to tide over the crisis without compromising the daily lives of people, he said, adding that it had ensured regular gas supply to homemakers.

title: EC seeks report on Mamata’s ‘provocative’ remark from CEO
content: With her statement seen as a provocation to TMC women workers and supporters to launch attacks at polling stations in rural areas, posing a risk to the central armed police forces deployed there, of facing criminal charges were they to intervene and stop them, EC will examine if the statement amounts of provocation and interferes in discharge of duties by poll and security personnel.

title: Top executives of two biggest US companies are leaving and they say the reason is AI
content: Two prominent CEOs, from Coca-Cola and Walmart, have cited the rise of AI as a factor in their decisions to step down. Both leaders indicated that the evolving business landscape, driven by AI, necessitates new leadership for future growth phases. Their departures highlight a significant technological shift impacting executive decision-making at major corporations.

title: Indian ships face GPS jams, mine alerts in Hormuz
content: Ships in the region are reporting repeated radio broadcasts over maritime channels warning vessels against transit. “Attention all ships… This is the Sepah Navy… navigation through Strait of Hormuz is forbidden… no ship is allowed to pass until further notice,” the radio messages repeatedly blare. Crews attributed these announcements to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy.

title: Govt clears Rs 2.4 lakh crore proposals for procurement of more S-400 units
content: The approval for additional S-400 ‘Sudarshan’ systems, which had helped neutralise Pakistani missiles during last year’s conflict in May and had also to its credit the world’s longest-range kill of an enemy aircraft, has come at a time when IAF teams are in Russia to receive the fourth squadron under the earlier contract for five such systems. The fourth system is likely to arrive by May while the fifth squadron is expected by Dec.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's Meta is rebranding employee titles and roles as it goes AI-native
content: Meta's Reality Labs is undergoing a significant internal overhaul, rebranding employees as 'AI builders' and forming smaller, AI-focused teams. This strategic shift aims to boost engineering productivity and product quality, with CEO Mark Zuckerberg envisioning AI dramatically transforming work.  These new 'AI pods' will foster cross-functional collaboration, with dedicated leads managing operations and performance.

title: Pilots of VIP charters can now refuse to fly in unsafe conditions
content: Noting that VVIPs “use air travel frequently for electioneering and other purposes in (small) aircraft” and choppers, DGCA said, “Analysis of earlier accidents/incidents associated with aircraft operations to/from airstrips/temporary helipads and during election flying has often revealed violation of instructions and safety was jeopardised. It is essential that adequate measures are taken by all concerned to ensure the highest standards of safety for operation of such flights.”

title: Tiger Woods faces another scary car crash before Masters decision
content: Tiger Woods was involved in a rollover car crash in Jupiter Island, Florida, on Friday afternoon. Early reports from witnesses suggest he appeared fine, but officials have not confirmed his condition yet. The incident is under investigation. The timing is important as Woods is expected to decide soon on playing in the Masters.

title: Commercial LPG allocation to states increased by 20%
content: In a letter to chief secretaries of states and UTs, petroleum secretary Neeraj Mittal said only those industrial units that have registered with oil marketing companies, declaring the end use of gas cylinders, and have also signed up for the piped gas network will be eligible for the additional quota of LPG. He added that if LPG is used by certain industrial units for special purposes that cannot be substituted by natural gas, such requirements would stand waived.

title: Fact Check: Was Clavicular Arrested for Viral Alligator Shooting Video
content: Kick streamer Braden “Clavicular” Peters went viral after a clip showed him allegedly shooting an alligator in Florida’s Everglades. The video sparked outrage and rumors of his arrest. However, no official confirmation links any arrest to the incident. Authorities are investigating, while reports suggest any detention may be unrelated, leaving key details unclear.

title: Candace Owens accuses Erika Kirk of plotting Charlie Kirk’s murder
content: Candace Owens has accused Erika of plotting her husband’s murder, alleging she tried to shape public perception in advance. Owens pointed to an Instagram post from September 8, where Erika shared a video of her frail mother, claiming it was meant to gain sympathy and build a narrative. The claims remain unverified and have sparked intense online debate.

title: Taylor Swift’s Artist of the Year win honored with Alysa Liu tribute
content: Olympic champion Alysa Liu shared a powerful on-stage moment with Taylor Swift at the iHeartRadio Music Awards, presenting her with Artist of the Year. Liu’s heartfelt tribute, calling Swift the “G.O.A.T. of music,” sparked emotional fan reactions. The crossover highlighted their shared journeys of independence, resilience, and redefining success on their own terms.

title: ‘Iran will never go nuclear, India can help end this war,’ says Khamenei’s representative
content: “Our supreme leader issued a fatwa. He said nuclear weapons are forbidden, haraam. So, we don't have it. We don't want to have it. We are not looking for it,” said Ilahi. Ending the war in West Asia depends on countries that started it, he said. He dismissed Pakistan’s claim that it was mediating between Iran and the US.

title: Defence, security at core of India-US ties: Sergio Gor
content: In an interview with the US embassy publication, Span magazine, the ambassador also spoke about the significance of Quad as he predicted further strengthening of defence ties. “India is a Major Defence Partner, and defence cooperation is one of the brightest spots in our bilateral relationship. We also strengthen US-India military interoperability through participation in military exercises such as Malabar, Tiger Triumph, and Cope India,” said Gor.

title: Supreme Court rebukes courts for naming rape survivors
content: A bench of Justices Sanjay Karol and N K Singh expressed concern that despite statutory safeguards and repeated directions from SC, such lapses persist. The court made the observation while convicting a rape accused, noting that the name of the minor survivor was mentioned in lower court orders. ​The court rejected the argument of the woman’s family.

title: Freedom of navigation important for global economic security: EAM Jaishankar
content: Jaishankar said on X he raised Global South’s concerns about energy challenges, fertiliser supplies and food security in the session on global governance. “The uncertainties arising from the conflict in West Asia only make a stronger case for more resilient trade corridors and supply chains,” he said. Jaishankar said on X India’s FTAs with the EU, EFTA members and the UK have enhanced the utility of IMEC.

title: Jan Vishwas bill moots fine, jail for grabbing govt land
content: The bill — introduced in Lok Sabha on Friday — proposes to amend Section-11 of the Public Premises (Eviction of Unauthorised Occupants) Act that at present provides for six months’ jail or with Rs 5,000 penalty or both. As per the proposal, the fine would be 5% of land value for every year of occupation or both — penalty and jail. Officials said this would deter encroachment and check land grabbing.

title: NCERT reset, AI push: Pradhan spells out education roadmap
content: On the recent controversy involving UGC regulations aimed at maintaining standards, quality and equity in Indian higher education, Pradhan refrained from detailed comment, noting the matter is sub judice. “The govt’s responsibility is to ensure that no one faces injustice or discrimination. We will abide by whatever directions court issues,” he said. Outlining the ministry’s broader priorities, Pradhan said the education system currently serves around 30 crore students, placing significant responsibility on policymakers.

title: ‘Evidence insufficient’: Spl Delhi court clears ex-MP Darda, son & others in coal ‘scam’ case
content: The case stemmed from the allocation in Maharashtra’s Bandar and led to the first CBI chargesheet in the larger coal block controversy dating back to the time Congress-led UPA was in office. The FIR was filed in 2012 and the chargesheet in 2014. Judge Sunena Sharma held that there was no proof of criminal conspiracy, noting the case was based on conjectures and lacked evidence of a “meeting of minds” or illegal agreement.

title: US, Ukraine citizens who went to Mizoram posed no threat to India: Shah
content: This is the first time a govt functionary has clarified on record that the group’s interest lay in Myanmar and India was merely a transit point. “No threat was posed to India’s security. It is our policy that any foreigner who visits India for any wrong act will not be spared,” he said.

title: 'Alive & protesting’: MP in Janata regime turns 100
content: Sitting in his Bharatpur home, the veteran, “Lohia’s oldest disciple” as he likes to call himself, speaks with the authority of one who has not just witnessed history, but shaped it. Until a recent fall briefly hospitalised him, he was holding three meetings a week. What keeps him going is unfinished work.

title: Diabetic drug metformin found to act on brain
content: A study published in Science Advances by Baylor College of Medicine has uncovered a hidden brain pathway that helps explain how metformin lowers blood sugar, offering fresh insight into a medicine used for over six decades. For years, metformin was believed to act mainly on the liver and gut. The new research shows it also works through the ventromedial hypothalamus — a small but crucial brain region that controls hunger, energy use and glucose balance.

title: From Kharg to Qeshm: 6 Iranian Islands Trump may target amid Strait of Hormuz tensions
content: The US is reportedly considering military options against Iran, focusing on key Persian Gulf islands vital for oil exports and naval control. Plans reportedly include ground and bombing operations, with potential targets like Kharg Island, a major oil hub, and Larak Island, crucial for Strait of Hormuz monitoring. Seizing these locations presents significant risks.

title: 'Trump getting bored with Iran, wants to move on': Report
content: President Trump is publicly portraying the US military involvement in Iran as over, despite ongoing troop deployments and high tensions. This disconnect from battlefield reality has unsettled aides and allies, with some describing his messaging as detached and driven by a desire to declare victory and move on.

title: Mirwaiz meets Iranian envoy, calls Kashmir ‘little Iran’
content: The delegation condemned the war, which it said was “imposed” on Iran, and expressed hope that diplomatic efforts would prevail and peace and stability would be restored. Others who were part of the delegation included Mufti Nasir-ul-Islam, Aga Syed Hassan Al-Mosvi, and Aga Syed Hadi AlMosvi, Aga Syed Mujtaba. During the meeting, Mirwaiz also highlighted the “deep-rooted cultural, historical and religious ties between Kashmir and Iran”.

title: 7 killed as avalanche buries several vehicles at Zojila Pass
content: The Zojila pass would earlier remain closed for nearly five months during winter, cutting off Ladakh from the rest of the country. However, with improved technology, advanced snow-clearance equipment and better planning, the pass remained open throughout this winter, barring four days, which the Border Roads Organisation described as a significant strategic and logistical achievement.

title: UK MP says US report on rise in anti-Indian hate online ‘deeply concerning’
content: Blackman, who represents Harrow East, where Hindus were recently attacked celebrating Holi, said of the report: “This is a coordinated campaign driven by a small number of accounts deliberately targeting the Indian community, its culture, and its contribution to public life. It is racism, plain and simple. The Indian diaspora here in Harrow, across the UK, and the world, makes an extraordinary contribution to our societies. That contribution deserves celebration. I will always stand against any form of discrimination targeting our Indian community."

title: Hydel power projects in J&K on fast track after Indus treaty abeyance: Omar
content: India last year placed the IWT in abeyance after the Pahalgam terror attack as part of a series of measures announced by PM Narendra Modi, including the suspension of the decades-old water-sharing pact. The treaty was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960 for sharing of Indus river system waters. According to the treaty, India got the full rights for utilization of waters of the three eastern rivers — Ravi, Beas and Satluj — while Pakistan got rights over the three western rivers, Indus, Chenab and Jhelum.

title: High-altitude unit spawned by Pahalgam attack, trained by AP’s Greyhounds
content: Snow Leopards were raised to strengthen high-altitude operations, including mountain tops and ridges, the official said. The Markhor batches are trained for forests and rugged mountainous areas long used by terrorists as hideouts. Its name is derived from a Persian term meaning “snake eater,” symbolising agility and resilience.  Both units are equipped with modern weapons and communication systems.

title: Sabrina Carpenter’s bold stance resurfaces as Shaquille O'Neal battles fake DM rumors
content: Shaquille O'Neal, the NBA legend, has found himself in a mess after he was accused of sending inappropriate texts to popular pop star Sabrina Carpenter.This news has sparked panic among fans as many troll Shaquille O'Neal for allegedly pursuing Sabrina Carpenter despite their massive age gap, with alleged inappropriate texts. Amid this, popstar Sabrina Carpenter’s powerful words have resurfaced on her moves in her concerts.

title: Fans react as Ayesha Curry’s 'disrespectful' comment about Stephen Curry
content: Ayesha Curry's innocent childhood anecdote about not wanting to date athletes, shared on a podcast, was misconstrued online as a present-day insult to husband Stephen Curry.  The internet's harsh reaction, fueled by a lack of context, transformed a lighthearted story about teenage misconceptions into a viral controversy, sparking debates about their marriage and Steph's choices.

title: Synergy, Team India spirit: What was discussed in key PM Modi-CMs meet amid Iran war
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi held a virtual meeting with Chief Ministers. The focus was on the Middle East conflict's impact on fuel supply, inflation, and logistics. Discussions aimed to ensure coordinated action between the Centre and states. Preparedness for potential disruptions and maintaining stability in essential sectors were key. Contingency planning for fuel availability and price pressures was also reviewed.

title: ‘Josh Hazlewood returns but…’: RCB coach gives key fitness update before SRH clash
content: RCB coach Andy Flower views the IPL's Impact Player rule as a positive tactical innovation, enhancing strategic decisions despite concerns about diluting team selection challenges.  He also confirmed Josh Hazlewood's arrival but unavailability for the opener, expressing confidence in other Indian pacers like Mangesh, Abhinandan, and Rasik to fill the void.

title: 'Rohit can’t be an Impact Player': Ex-India batter sends strong message to Hardik
content: Mohammad Kaif urges Mumbai Indians to fully utilize Rohit Sharma on the field, not just as an Impact Player. He believes Rohit's leadership and on-field presence are crucial, especially in high-pressure situations, and would benefit both the team and his own batting. Kaif emphasized that a fitter Rohit should be involved in all game phases.

title: Bodycam shows Sherrone Moore crying during arrest after Michigan firing, allegations
content: Sherrone Moore was seen crying in newly released body cam footage from his December arrest in Michigan. The former coach spoke about losing his job and struggling emotionally. His arrest followed serious allegations involving Paige Shiver. Moore later accepted a plea deal on reduced charges, and his sentencing is scheduled for April 14.

title: Tiger arrested on suspicion of DUI after crash
content: Tiger Woods was arrested on suspicion of DUI following a car crash Friday in which he struck another vehicle and rolled his Land Rover, authorities said.

title: Sources: Sun sold, will relocate to Houston in '27
content: The Connecticut Sun franchise is being sold to the Fertitta family for a record $300 million to bring the WNBA back to Houston, sources confirmed to ESPN on Friday.

title: McCollum says he will remain as Iowa's coach
content: With the possibility of multiple high-profile jobs being open this offseason, Iowa head coach Ben McCollum said Friday that he will return to the Hawkeyes in 2026-27.

title: Hidalgo hailed by ND after near quadruple-double
content: Hannah Hidalgo posts a triple-double and sets the record for the most steals in Division I history as Notre Dame knocks off Vanderbilt to reach the Elite Eight.

title: Ole Miss QB Chambliss clears hurdle to play in '26
content: A significant legal hurdle for Ole Miss quarterback Trinidad Chambliss to play in 2026 was cleared Friday.

title: Raphinha ruled out 5 weeks in blow to Barcelona
content: Barcelona forward Raphinha faces five weeks on the sideline after suffering a hamstring injury while on international duty, the club have confirmed.

title: Florida AG challenges NFL to abolish Rooney Rule
content: Florida's attorney general has sent a letter to NFL commissioner Roger Goodell to express concern that the Rooney Rule is "blatant race and sex discrimination" and that hiring decisions should be based on merit only.

title: 🏈 NFL draft buzz: Simpson's pro day and more
content: How high could QB Ty Simpson get picked? Is Jeremiyah Love really a possibility for the Titans at No. 4?

title: Wetzel: Despite scandals, Rick Pitino might be the best men's college hoops coach ever
content: Less than 10 years ago, Pitino's career seemed over. But after multiple scandals, he's back doing what he always does, winning in the NCAA tournament.

title: Why NBA insiders are all-in on the Spurs: 'Victor breaks all the rules'
content: San Antonio's title buzz is growing around the league. Plus, new intel on expansion and an East playoff race full of injury questions.

title: NHL playoff watch: Projecting the Red Wings' postseason chances
content: Detroit is running out of time to climb back into a playoff spot. Plus, full postseason bracket projections.

title: 'Nobody has seen the best in Vladdy yet': How a dominant October took Vladimir Guerrero Jr. to a new level
content: Toronto's slugger proved he was a superstar in the playoffs, but it was in his leadership that he really stepped up. Now, the hope is that carries over to 2026 -- and beyond.

title: WNBA's CBA: 10 biggest wins from new agreement
content: The CBA permanently and positively changes the WNBA's landscape. These are the wins, biggest strides and areas of compromise.

title: How every Sweet 16 game was won Thursday -- and each winner's keys to Elite Eight
content: Arizona, Illinois, Iowa and Purdue are moving on. Here's how they advanced -- and how they can do it again.

title: Reranked: Who is the MVP for each of the top Premier League clubs?
content: Based on a simple combination of ability and tactical importance, we've reranked the best players in order at the Big Six Premier League clubs.

title: How Hodgson's 44-year coaching gap at one club compares to others in soccer, NFL, NBA
content: Roy Hodgson is returning to Bristol City as their interim manager, 44 years after they hired him the first time.

title: This is the deepest USMNT squad maybe ever. Is it enough for the best World Cup result ever?
content: World Cup-winning teams need more than their starting 11, but the U.S. men's national team never had enough good bench options. Maybe that has finally changed?

title: A former NFL GM's must-draft board for 2026: Tannenbaum pounds the table for 14 prospects
content: From Dillon Thieneman on Day 1 to Jaffer Murphy late on Day 3, Mike Tannenbaum picks the draft prospects he'd want to target across all seven rounds.

title: Lehigh Valley IronPigs poke fun at Zack Wheeler's rib removal in rehab promo
content: The Philadelphia Phillies' ace had a rib removed as part of a surgery to remove a blood clot -- and kept it in his closet.

title: 'The KING ... Shot by ME': Caitlin Clark photographs LeBron James at Lakers-Pacers
content: The Fever star was behind the lens as a credentialed photographer at Wednesday's Lakers-Pacers matchup.

title: Women's college hoops: UConn consolidates status as odds-on title favorite
content: Odds for each team to advance through the bracket and to win the 2026 women's college basketball championship.

title: Ted Ginn Jr., AJ McCarron and Chris Redman begin pro coaching careers in familiar spots
content: The UFL comes out swinging in Columbus, Louisville and Birmingham with familiar college stars as coaches.

title: Spiky and unforgettable, Wales went 'full gas' but paid the price
content: Wales scrapped for every inch of the pitch against Bosnia and Herzegovina, but in the end, it wasn't enough to save their World Cup dream.

title: World Cup playoff preview, predictions: Who will book the final six spots?
content: The final six spots -- four from Europe and two from the intercontinental playoff -- will be clinched this week.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Could bumper new Atlético deal keep Álvarez away from Arsenal, Barça?
content: Julián Álvarez has been linked with Arsenal and Barcelona, but Atlético Madrid are preparing a big pay day to keep him. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Draft guide: Rankings, tips, strategy, stats and more
content: All the rankings, projections, cheat sheets, strategy and analysis you need for the fantasy baseball season.

title: "We're Negotiating Now": Trump Jokingly Calls Hormuz "Strait Of Trump"

title: Fertilizer stock ample, working to ensure supply intact: Chouhan
content:
Union minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan (ANI photo)

NEW DELHI: India has sufficient stock of fertilisers and govt is working to ensure supply remains intact, Union agriculture minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan said Friday. “Whenever there is war, it’s a crisis for the entire world and has a direct and indirect impact on every country. I will not say there is no problem. But we have done all preparations to deal with it. As of today, we have adequate stock of fertilisers. Currently we are not worried and are working on plans to ensure it remains sufficient,” Chouhan said. He stressed govt will leave no stone unturned to ensure options remain available even during a prolonged conflict. Chouhan said there is a need to reduce burden on people in agriculture as landholdings are small, but noted many young people using technology are turning to farming. “Many farmers have doubled their income. Some have even tripled or quadrupled it,” he added.

End of Article
